So here's a quick thing that may help some people close abandoned hopes or a week three way ins. And it's sort of like I hate to use the word steamroll because like the last thing I want to do is steamroll someone who's not qualified or somebody who's not gonna be a good member. But if I can kind of steamroll the process where I know they would or should be staying anyway, then what the heck not, right? So check this out. It's really the same thing that I've always been doing. The only difference now is in the delivery method, the way I say certain things, the words I use and the fact that I kind of don't even ask them. I don't even let them talk. I just kind of explain to them what's going on, act all excited and show them how they're gonna benefit from this. And I just keep nodding and they just keep nodding along with me and I congratulate them and they just signed it and boom, done. So here's how it works real quick. Don't worry about the numbers so much. Just follow along with it. I'm gonna go a little faster with you guys just for the sake of the video that I normally would with. I'm gonna speak faster and write faster than I normally would with a prospect. The entire thing is about five to ten minutes at best. So what I do is they come in. I don't care if they gained 30 pounds, lost five percent. It doesn't matter. None of that is relevant. I congratulate them or I explain to them what we need to do to fix the stalling or whatever it is that they don't feel that they're hidden. The one thing they all have in common is they love the program, right? And it's up to us as a gym to do the right thing to make sure these people are happy, make sure that they're getting good results, and make sure that the coaches are upbeat and keeping it fun. If you're doing that, this part of your job is super easy. Like there's no way someone's leaving if you're keeping them happy. You just kind of have to show them that they're not leaving, right? Because they may not know it yet. So here's how it goes. Alright, so let's say we're past the point now of Let's say we're past the point of these guys agreeing that, okay, we can help you. Won't let me. Alright. Follow along. Here's what we're gonna do, Mr. or Mrs. Prospect, right? So look, you've essentially put in $600 for a six-week program, right? That's a rate of $100 per week, and in this particular challenge, it expires on April 1st. What does that mean? What's that expiration date? Simple. It means that you have to hit that goal of let's say 6% body fat by April 1st. If you don't, you pay for the program. If you do, you get a rebate to use for your membership, right? So check it out. You've paid a rate of $100 a week. Very simple. If you told me, I want it to stay because I love this program so much, which I know you do because you just told me. So take that $100 a week and stay for another 52 weeks, right? That's a whole year. That means you owe me $5,200 according to what we just did. But hold on, right? Before you get all freaked out, check it out. Nobody pays $5,200 for the year. You know why? Because when I can budget for a full year knowing that you're going to be here, I give you a tremendous discount. I give you like 55% off. People are paying us $2,400 for a year to get everything you just received and more. Why more? Because instead of three days a week, you now have unlimited access and you have the whole community of our member base and not just the beginners, right? So for $2,400, you've got the whole year. So that's a tremendous discount, right? But hold on, check this out. Here's what I'm going to do for you next. You don't have to come in right now and write me a check for $2,400. You know why? Because you gave me $600 for this program, right? And you have to hit a go by April 1st. But because I know you're already committed and you're here at the midway point, right? This was a dangling carrot. This was to keep you in check. Make sure that you're accountable. Make sure you're doing the right things. But you've already shown you have the last three weeks that you're here, you're engaged, you're doing the program, you're showing up for classes, and you're following the meal plan. So what I need to do is make sure that you continue to do that. But I no longer need to hold a financial cloud over your head. So what I want to do right now is a thank you for staying. I'm going to take that $600 and I want to put it right here. So I'm actually going to give you that program for free. So check it out. That $2,400 just became $1,800 because I'm kicking $600 back from my money back to you. Now you can come in right now and write me a check for $1,800 and we're done for the year. But I understand if that is a little too much. So instead of coming in at a monthly rate of $2,29, which is what our month to month members do, right?
